title: ComfyUI Tutorial Series Ep 28: Create Consistent Characters with Flux + Train Loras Online
description:
Learn how to create consistent characters using ComfyUI and Flux in this detailed tutorial. In episode 28, I guide you through generating character sheets, training Loras online, and using them locally for customized character generation. This step-by-step guide covers everything from setting up workflows to using advanced tools like OpenPose, ControlNet, and upscalers to refine your results.

Get the workflows and instructions from discord
https://discord.gg/gggpkVgBf3

Check Other Episodes
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL-pohOSaL8P9kLZP8tQ1K1QWdZEgwiBM0

Lora used for Eyes
https://civitai.com/models/850406?modelVersionId=951461
Download it and place it in the Loras Folder

Unlock exclusive perks by joining our channel:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCmMbwA-s3GZDKVzGZ-kPwaQ/join

#comfyui #fluxai #aitutorial 

Chapters:
00:00 Introduction
00:27 Generate Images for Lora Training - Character Sheet
17:53 Lora Online Training
26:12 Generate Images with Your Lora
transcript: Welcome to episode 28 of our Comfy UI tutorial series.  Today I will show you how I create consistent characters. I tried to get more accurate poses  using the apply control net settings,  but the image quality suffered,  so I tried to find a balance.  However, the rest of the poses worked fine.  So make your own reference image with your own poses  to see what works best for you.  I am using a fixed seed,  and you'll see later why.  If you want to generate a new image,  simply increase or decrease the seed and generate again.  For example, I got this character sheet.  It looks okay, but the girl's poses aren't exactly what I'm looking for.  Keep in mind that this will go into lower training.  So if you don't like a pose and it's included in the training,  some of your generated images might end up with that pose.  So only include the poses you like in your training.  I like the previous one.  So I'll go back to that seed.  Now, the image is too small for training.  I need four different images at 10, 24 pixels.  So I'm using an upscaler.  I've made it easy for you.  Just enable the upscaler from here,  and that will activate this workflow.  I'm scaling down the image.  Because if I leave it at 10, 24 pixels,  the upscale that goes to K sampler will be 20, 48 pixels,  which is too much for flux,  as it can only handle a maximum of two megapixels.  So try downscaling to 832 or 704 pixels.  First, we get a 2x upscale,  which is sometimes good enough for what you need.  But I've also included a 4x upscale,  which can sometimes be sharper than the 2x upscale.  However, for some images, it might be too sharp  or introduce artifacts.  So check both versions in your output folder  and use the one that looks best to you.  Since the seed on the first K sampler was fixed,  it will only run the upscaler.  Sometimes the upscaled version might have some errors  or correct some of the issues from the first generation.  That's why I used a random seed for the second K sampler  and set the denoise to 0.95  so it can introduce more variation  and subtle changes to the original.
